what_it_does: A browser-side generator library that produces reusable SVG and HTML organism graphics—icons, network meshes, hub/spoke diagrams, phase strips, charts, file-system graphs, connectors, taxonomy diagrams, and synthetic artefact thumbnails—colored only by CSS custom properties and rendered deterministically from data.
observations: Attaches itself as DNA.org inside an IIFE with 'use strict'.
Contains a hand-coded 24x24 icon path set (P) with ten icons and a default ledger fallback.
Uses a seeded mulberry32 PRNG so mesh and hub layouts are reproducible.
Every public function returns an SVG or HTML string rather than a DOM node.
Colors come exclusively from CSS variables such as --gold, --bronze-line, --page, and --ochre.
graph() is the most complex organism: a multi-column containment-tree visualizer with folder orbit dots, state sockets, and geometry export via o.out.
Several local helpers (esc, trim, clip) are redefined in multiple places instead of shared.
Some organisms depend on injected dependencies: iconSVG, typeHue, and DNA.ordinal.
Newer artefact-family functions include aria-label and role=img attributes.
Inline style attributes are baked into generated HTML/SVG strings.
standouts: graph() encodes a folder's child composition as colored orbit dots and exports layout geometry for external camera control.
connector() renders a plug/socket authorization motif with gradients and an octagonal gold core.
taxonomyEmergence() visualizes provisional kinds giving way to real kinds discovered from coverage.
hubNetwork() uses a label-fit sizing rule so circle radius adapts to text length while score affects proximity and stroke weight.
checkMatrix() turns boolean comparisons into a gold-check / muted-red-cross table with a highlighted self column.
conventions: Options object with defaults: o = o || {}.
Deterministic seeded randomness for spatial layouts.
ARIA labels on newer artefact generators.
concerns: Inline style attributes in generated strings may conflict with CSP or theming.
Repeated local definitions of esc/trim/clip indicate duplication.
graph() mixes layout, rendering, and a side-effect geometry export.
Hard-coded font families assume specific typefaces are loaded.
Some functions reference global DNA without visible guards.
notes: This file is the visual grammar layer: deterministic, token-colored SVG/HTML generators that turn data into branded organism graphics without external images.
questions: Which module injects iconSVG and typeHue into graph()?
Where are the CSS token definitions that these variables reference?
How does graph-view consume the geometry exported via o.out?
Are the later artefact-family functions consumed by composition.js or by specific page scripts?
How is the seed chosen per instance to avoid identical visuals across pages?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to generate the organism layer and drafted icon set; the file does exactly that, and has expanded into a broad family of diagram and artefact generators.
apparent_themes: Synthetic material density without real assets.
Containment and emergence as spatial diagrams.
Progressive disclosure and state sockets in graphs.
intention_signals: The file is trying to establish a self-contained visual language where every UI organism can be generated from data and tokens, replacing bespoke illustrations with programmable, live graphics.
novelty: Folder orbit dots that encode child-type composition, a prior-to-real taxonomy emergence diagram, and a plug/socket authorization connector motif are unusual for a typical component library.
contribution: A deterministic, token-colored generator library that produces icons, network diagrams, charts, comparison matrices, file-system graphs, and synthetic artefact thumbnails, letting pages render branded visuals from data without external image assets.
summary_for_embedding: organisms.js is a browser-side generator library of SVG and HTML organism components—icons, meshes, hub networks, charts, graphs, connectors, and synthetic artefacts—colored by CSS design tokens and rendered deterministically from data.
intention_for_embedding: To provide reusable, token-driven visual primitives that let the surface layer generate branded diagrams and artefact thumbnails programmatically rather than relying on static images.

symbols.does: Renders a single drafted icon as an SVG string.
symbols.description: Uses the P path map and ICON constants to build a 24x24 SVG with token stroke color, scalable size, and a default ledger fallback when the name is unknown.

symbols.does: Renders a row of icons on a dashed rail.
symbols.description: Places each icon horizontally with configurable size and gap, adds a dashed baseline with drop-lines, and optionally draws a descend line ending in a gold plug marker.
Returns a closure that produces floats in [0,1) from a 32-bit seed, keeping mesh and hub layouts reproducible across renders.
Generates n random points seeded by o.seed, links each node to its two nearest forward neighbors, and colors nodes gold-to-bronze by distance from a configurable heart point.

symbols.does: Local color mapper for mesh nodes.
symbols.description: Computes the normalized distance from a point to the heart and maps it to --gold, --ochre, or --bronze.

symbols.does: Renders a typed hub with directional spokes.
symbols.description: Arranges labeled nodes radially around a hexagonal or octagonal hub, sizes circles to fit labels uniformly, and varies proximity, opacity, and stroke weight by optional score.

symbols.does: Renders a compact stats panel as HTML.
symbols.description: Builds a rounded, token-colored div with a small-caps title and rows of figure/label pairs, optionally starring the first row in gold.

symbols.does: Renders a vertical time-spine stage diagram.
symbols.description: Draws a dashed spine with stage boxes, date labels, and connecting elbows, all in token colors.

symbols.does: Renders a tiny monospace texture line.
symbols.description: Joins an array of entries with middots in a small monospace, faint-colored HTML span.
Builds connected arrow segments where the active index is filled with goldwash and highlighted in gold while inactive segments are muted.
Computes the notched polygon and centered text for one phaseStrip segment, applying active or inactive token styling.

symbols.does: Renders a quote block with gold quotation marks.
symbols.description: Wraps a quote and attribution in an optional gold-bordered frame with large gold quotation glyphs.
Builds an HTML table with gold checkmarks, muted-red crosses, and a highlighted self column on goldwash.
Returns a goldwash background CSS string when the column index matches the highlighted self column.
Builds concentric-ring segments from values, colors them by ordinal or explicit color, and optionally places a centered label.
Draws normalized bars with optional ordinal coloring and configurable bar width, gap, and height.
Delegates to DNA.ordinal if present, otherwise samples a fixed gold-to-bronze ramp by index.

Lays out folder/file columns along the screen's long axis, draws folder orbit dots colored by child type, traces between active parents and children, and exports geometry via o.out for external camera control.
Returns the children array for a given address from tree.childrenOf, defaulting to empty.
Replaces &, <, >, and the quote character with entities; local helper inside graph.
Shortens strings over 18 characters while preserving the extension so similar filenames remain distinguishable.
Computes the coordinate of a graph column along the depth axis from margin, column index, and constants.
Sorts children by kind and type, places colored dots clockwise around the folder icon in stacked rings, and adds a radial divider between directory and file dots.
Assembles a translated group containing a disc, optional type icon, orbit dots, selection ring, and label with state-driven opacity and stroke.
Draws a quadratic bezier between nodes with a muted or gold arrowhead and optional animated dash class.

symbols.does: Local filtered and sorted child list builder.
symbols.description: Filters children by query and sorts directories before files by id inside graph.
Computes a sibling's position centered under the active parent or from the page margin.
Draws a bronze socket ring, dashed bronze-to-gold transport line, gold prongs, and an octagonal authorization core with a bolt glyph and radial glow.
Shows dashed ghost pills for provisional labels on the left, filled emerged pills on the right, and dashed transition arrows with gold heads.
Same entity-replacement helper as the earlier esc, defined locally for taxonomyEmergence.
Computes a pill's center y coordinate for a given index and step size in taxonomyEmergence.
Builds a tile grid of warm cover bands and caption lines with one highlighted tile, using token gradients and an aria-label.
Generates a varied cover thumbnail and two caption lines for a designLibraryObject tile based on its index.

symbols.does: Renders a stack of document pages.
symbols.description: Draws offset pages back-to-front with a warm header band, content lines, and a single gold dog-ear on the front page.
Draws a grid, poché walls, a partition wall with door gap, and a gold focal marker on a paper gradient.
Returns a filled rectangle representing an architectural wall segment.

symbols.does: Renders a grid of project tiles.
symbols.description: Part of the artefact family; generates a tiled project overview from tokens. Implementation is not visible in the provided excerpt.
Part of the artefact family; likely shows layered document or plan versions as a stack. Implementation is not visible in the provided excerpt.
Part of the artefact family; likely combines a real or synthetic source thumbnail with metadata chrome. Implementation is not visible in the provided excerpt.
Part of the artefact family; likely a horizontal strip of evidence items or badges. Implementation is not visible in the provided excerpt.
Part of the artefact family; likely a synthetic UI component preview such as a card or control panel. Implementation is not visible in the provided excerpt.
Part of the artefact family; likely shows artefacts moving through stages or connections. Implementation is not visible in the provided excerpt.
Part of the artefact family; likely groups multiple artefact thumbnails in a small composition. Implementation is not visible in the provided excerpt.
Returns an absolute-positioned HTML div string with transform, shadow, and z-index for artefact cluster layouts.
Part of the artefact family; likely a kanban-style board grouping visual. Implementation is not visible in the provided excerpt.
Another local entity-escaping helper, defined later in the file.

symbols.does: Maps state strings to token colors.
symbols.description: Returns var(--gold) for living/active/live/open/pending states, otherwise var(--secondary).

symbols.does: Renders a star or constellation diagram.
symbols.description: Likely draws a network of status stars connected by traces. Implementation is not visible in the provided excerpt.
Yet another local entity-escaping helper near the constellation function.
Shortens strings exceeding n characters by appending an ellipsis.

symbols.does: Maps status strings to token colors.
symbols.description: Returns gold-pop for supervised-live/live/active, bronze for closed, and ochre otherwise.
Returns an SVG or HTML star element with optional glow, color, and label.
Likely builds a vertical or horizontal rail of icon or button entries with token styling. Implementation is not visible in the provided excerpt.
Final local entity-escaping helper near navRail.
